RED_COST_01.why: context: value figure (statutory £751/wk cap, £22,530 max, age-banded formula) — no marginal; observed average cost per employee remains unanchored

RED_TERM_02.why: context: value figure (statutory cap £751/wk from Apr 2026, 30 weeks max) — no marginal; enhanced-offer distribution unanchored; seeded bands already Check-C fl

REW26_BEN_PLSA_QM.why: context: upper-bound proxy (<=0.29) — bound cannot set a target_share unless David rules to seed at the bound

REW26_BEN_SALSAC.why: do-not-emit: 0.54 is large-only AND measures offer-at-all, not default enrolment; if David rules the question means 'offered', blend 0.15/0.54 (pair on REW_BEN_

REW_BEN_038.why: CONTEXT not a single prevalence: this anchor is a compendium of ~15 separate benefit prevalences (each with its own all/large/SME split), i.e. a distribution across options of a multi-select question. Usable only if split into one structured row per benefit option; several entries are themselves ranges (e.g. PMI SME 17-18%) or large-only (e.g. free eye tests 52% large). Do not collapse to one number. Note internal near-duplicates with other rows (e.g. OSP 60% large here vs 60-66% in REW_BEN_SICK_001; EAP 10% all / 61-62% large here vs 67/78/39 in REW26_WEL_EAP - conflicting EAP figures across register rows, reviewer should reconcile sources).

REW_BEN_048.why: CONTEXT not a prevalence: 50-59% is a benefit LEVEL band (percent of salary), and 25.2% is the share of the conditional base (employers already offering IP) sitting in that modal band - conditional distribution, not an all-employer target share. The 34% 'offer no IP' figure is corroboration context only and conflicts in direction of expression with REW_BEN_046 (25% offer vs 34% offer none - different sources/bases, broker-book skew per base_field). Grade B, not nationally weighted.

REW_BEN_112.why: CONTEXT only: 68% is the share of employers inside a 3-6% contribution RANGE — a distribution statistic for a categorical level question, likely spanning multiple answer options, so unusable as a single target share. Broker-book skew toward larger/advised employers per base_field. Grade B.

REW_BEN_PENS_EE_MAX_01.why: CONTEXT only: all figures are contribution-rate LEVELS (median 6%, p25 5%, p75 7%, range 3-12%), not employer-prevalence percentages — the % signs denote salary percentages, not shares of employers. Tiny base (n=59) with large-employer/FTSE skew and pre-2024 vintage per base_field. Grade B.

REW_BEN_PENS_EMP_MAX_01.why: CONTEXT only: two OVERLAPPING cumulative thresholds (~50% at 9%+, 27% at 11%+; the 11%+ group is a subset of the 9%+ group) — these are CDF points for a banded level question, not option shares, and cannot seed a single marginal without the reviewer choosing a threshold. '~' approximate on the 50. Broker-book skew toward larger/advised employers per base_field. Grade B.

REW263_TIME_NEONATAL.why: context: statutory entitlement value (up to 12 wks from Apr 2025) - enhancement prevalence unmeasured; no marginal

REW263_TIME_PATPAY.why: context: self-selected benchmark-tier base - 0.67 is Bright Horizons top-tier (family-friendly benchmark participants), 0.21 broad measures a different quantity

REW263_TIME_PREGLOSS.why: context: legal-horizon note (statutory pregnancy-loss leave from 2027 under ERA 2025, currently discretionary; incoming right is UNPAID while the question asks

REW_BEN_FAM_006.why: context: statutory-floor values (SMP/SPP/ShPP GBP194.32/wk, SMP 6wks at 90% AWE, paternity 2wks, SPL 50wks, day-one rights) - legal baseline, no enhancement pre

REW_BEN_HOL_002.why: context: statutory-floor value (5.6 weeks / 28 days; employers MAY count the 8 bank holidays within it) - legal baseline, no prevalence measured; no marginal

REW_BEN_HOL_004.why: context: collective-agreement base (IDR Payline, 56/98 = 0.57; unionised, 84% 250+ FTE, ~51% public/voluntary) - unblendable subgroup on an all-cohort question;

REW_BEN_SICK_002.why: NOT a prevalence: duration distribution (~10% under 1 month, ~40% over 6 months) with conditional denominator 'orgs with OSP', approximate '~' figures, bands do not sum to 100. Question itself asks a duration ('for how long'), not a yes/no. 'Public sector most generous' is qualitative. Route to CONTEXT.

PROP_634adacd.why: NOT a prevalence: 3% is the median EXPECTED basic pay increase (forward-looking level/value). Seeding it as an employer share would be a category error. Route to CONTEXT for the salary-increase question's answer distribution.

PROP_9e4ad87f.why: context: value figure (pay-AWARD median 3%; band split 3-3.99% = 40% of employers, 5%+ = 15%) — award distribution, not budget-as-%-of-payroll; no marginal

PROP_dff9a2a5.why: context: value figure (~3% is median award SIZE from CIPD LMO Spring 2026, not a coverage share; '<0%/freeze small tail' is directional only) — no marginal

REW_BEN_058.why: context: forward-looking benefits-SPEND direction split (plan increase 8% / same 73% / decrease 6%), not a retrospective enhanced-in-last-12m prevalence — no ma

REW_PAY_HOURLY_MIN_1c6e096f.why: context: value figure (statutory floor — NLW 21+ £12.21 Apr 2025 -> £12.71 Apr 2026; NMW 18-20 £10.85) — left-tail boundary constraint only, no marginal

REW_INC_103.why: context: employee-level receipt base — 12.2% of ALL UK employees received a bonus (yr to May 2025; small firms 20.4% vs large 7.8%) — receipt is not eligibility

REW_INC_104.why: context: value/band distribution on the wrong denominator — payout as % of TARGET (90-100% band = 60% i.e. 26/43; 71-90% ≈ 23%) against a question denominated i

REW_INC_131.why: context: subset-scope prevalence — AESP ~16% all-UK covers all-employee share plans only, and 21-24% is an among-performance-linked-employers base; neither maps

REW_INC_133.why: context: staff-category participation splits from NON-LTI schemes (team bonus MPT 37% vs other 23%; profit-share 32% vs 19%; AESP ~16%) — no LTI-eligibility-by-

REW263_WEL_MGRTRAIN.why: context: org-level base (29% of orgs train line managers) on a manager-denominator coverage question; no marginal unless a trained-manager-proportion distributi

REW26_WEL_BUDGET.why: numeric, no distribution anchor (G8) — 0.54 large-only exists-rate carried as documentation

EXT_REW_GAP_006.why: context: non-quantitative modal claim ('most commonly Financial Award or Gift', legacy n=42, no percentages) - cannot calibrate a distribution; no marginal. Fla

REW263_REC_PEER.why: context: superset-scope figure - 0.66 is any-recognition-scheme prevalence (legacy platform 2019-20, n=74, large/FTSE skew), an upper bound only for peer-to-pee

REW263_GOV_MENOPLAN.why: context: proxy-with-caveat (policy != action plan) — ruled context-only 2026-07-16
